flares.note: End time is cut off by a small, but visually distinct in GOES SUVI 131, separate C3.2 peak from AR 14401 (N23E65) starting at 2026-03-23T00:24Z that peaks at 2026-03-23T00:29Z.
Double-peaked flare preceding a C3.7 flare peaking at 2026-03-25T00:30Z.

cmes.note: This somewhat asymmetric bright CME is visible to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 coronagraph and for a couple of frames before the usual overnight data gap in STEREO A COR2. Its source is a significant filament eruption with associated brightening/dimming starting around 2026-03-22T15:30Z, followed by high-rising post-eruptive arcades, as seen in GOES SUVI 304/284/195/131 centered around S20E15, south of Active Region 14398, and seen as ejecta and opening of field lines in STEREO A EUV 195/403 on/close to the SE limb. It appears that there may be some SE deflection/directionality to the CME as it erupts and progresses outward in the GOES SUVI 284 field of view. There is a bright bulk front and a wider faint shock front seen in all three coronagraphs.

cmeAnalyses.note: Final analysis of this CME with later imagery from SOHO LASCO and GOES CCOR-1 and with a few early timestamps of STEREO A COR2 before the routine evening data gap.

Analysis of the fainter wider shock front based on the fit with the imagery from SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs, including the early imagery from STEREO A COR2 before the usual evening data gap and fully available SOHO LASCO imagery.
PRELIMINARY MEASUREMENT USING FIRST FEW INITIAL FRAMES. Parameters based on best fit using three frames of SOHO LASCO C2 imagery and four frames of STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery.

cmes.note: Faint loop CME seen in difference imagery of SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 but covered by a daily data gap in STEREO A COR2. No source was found for it in EUV imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: The CME is covered by the daily data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery and no source has been found for it in EUV imagery. Only plane-of-sky analysis is possible.

cmes.note: Faint CME seen to the W in SOHO LASCO and GOES CCOR-1 imagery but in STEREO A COR2 it is covered by the daily data gap. No source for it has been found in EUV imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: The CME is covered by the daily data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery and no source has been found for it in EUV imagery. Only plane-of-sky analysis is possible.

cmes.note: CME observed to the W in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, also W in STEREO A COR2 after a data gap ends at 2026-03-23T03:38Z. The source is a wide eruption from AR 14392 (S17W65), associated with a C1.5 flare peaking at 2026-03-22T23:09Z as observed in GOES SUVI 131. A small filament eruption near AR 14392 begins at 2026-03-22T22:50Z in GOES SUVI 304, setting off a wider rising and breaking of field lines over AR 14392 and likely extending around the west limb, starting around 2026-03-22T23:34Z in GOES SUVI 171/195/284. This is followed by a post-eruptive arcade over AR 14392 observed in GOES SUVI 171/195/284/304.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulated bulk analysis with SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. Due to the distorted lobed shape of the CME front, this analysis best captures the faster northern leading edge of the CME front using the full width of the bright bulk material. The CME front is further distorted in STEREO A COR2's field-of-view, but able to be captured good enough to track with the other coronagraphs.

SHOCK analysis using only SOHO LASCO C3, as the shock front is not resolvable in STEREO A COR2 imagery potentially due to faintness, being directed towards the spacecraft, and/or the COR2A data gap ending at 2026-03-23T03:38Z. The longitude could vary up to W55-W85, the source longitude of W65 is used as a best approximation with available imagery.

cmes.note: CME observed to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 and as a partial halo to the S/SW in STEREO A COR2. This CME immediately follows the larger CME 2026-03-22T23:24Z, and it is very likely outflow or a streamer blowout following that eruption from AR 14392 (S17W65), corroborated via coronagraph triangulation in the swpc_cat tool. The field line loops over AR 14392, which initially began to erupt around 2026-03-22T23:34Z in GOES SUVI 171/195/284, seen as a second movement of field lines starting around 2026-03-23T01:31Z. That first eruption is associated with a C1.5 flare as observed in GOES SUVI 131, which is not linked to this second CME.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2, using later frames only due to observed acceleration when including early frames. The CME shape is very distorted in STEREO A COR2 compared to SOHO LASCO, appearing much wider. Using STEREO A COR2's width, the leading edge still tracks very well between COR2 and C3.

cmes.note: Narrow, faint, jet-like CME visible to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. There is no clear source for this CME.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the SW only in GOES CCOR-1. SOHO LASCO is in a downlink gap, and the CME may be too faint or directed towards STEREO A COR2. No distinct source for this CME is observed, but based on its direction and recent eruptions it may be an outflow from AR 14392 following significant CMEs 2026-03-22T23:24Z and 2026-03-23T02:36Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: GOES CCOR-1 only plane-of-sky due to no other coronagraphs observing this front in real-time and unobserved source.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the West by STEREO A COR2 beginning at 2026-03-24T03:53 following the end of a data gap which began at 2026-03-23T20:23Z. This CME is not visible in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 or GOES CCOR-1, as it is likely completely far sided with respect to the Sun-Earth line. This event is also quite faint in STERO A COR2 and is therefore only visible for a few frames. Due to the data gap in STEREO A and the far sided nature of the CME, no clear source is seen for this event.

cmeAnalyses.note: STEREO A plane-of-sky using a longitude of W143.8 due to lack of visible source and usable coronagraph imagery to triangulate.

cmes.note: Narrow, faint CME first seen to the SW by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-03-24T12:12Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. The source of this event is a very small eruption seen beginning at approx. 2026-03-24T11:12Z located at approx. S40W35 as seen in GOES SUVI 195.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: CME seen to the NE in GOES CCOR-1 and SOHO LASCO C2/C3. The CME is not visible in STEREO A COR2 in real-time due to a data gap starting at 2026-03-24T13:53Z. The source is a C7.2 flare starting at 2026-03-24T17:18Z as seen in GOES SUVI 131 imagery. Opening field lines and post-eruptive arcades are also visible near the NE limb in GOES SUVI 171/195/284/304 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Updated measurement with additional coronagraph imagery. Measurement based on the source location. The longitude is approximate based on location near the east limb.
Measurement based on the source location. The longitude is approximate due to the source location near the east limb.

cmes.note: CME visible to the southwest in GOES CCOR-1 and in limited available SOHO LASCO C3 imagery in real-time during a data downlink period. Not visible in STEREO A COR2 imagery due to a data gap starting at 2026-03-24T13:53Z. The source is a C2.9 flare and associated eruption from AR 14400 (approximately S14W52) starting at 2026-03-24T23:52Z as seen in GOES SUVI 195/284 with rapidly opening field lines and possible EUV wave.

cmeAnalyses.note: Updated parameters based on SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery with matching to possible features in STEREO A COR2 following the data gap. Possible deflection within the FOV due to nearby CH.
Preliminary measurement based on the source location using 3 frames of available GOES CCOR-1 and SOHO LASCO C3 imagery.

cmes.note: Faint CME visible to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Blocked in CCOR-1 by Earth-shine. No clear source but appears to be within the outflow of the CME: 2026-03-24T20:15Z (CCOR-1).

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: Faint CME visible to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A, and possibly in GOES CCOR-1 following the Earth-shine event. This CME follows in the same path of the CMEs: 2026-03-24T20:15Z (CCOR-1) and 2026-03-25T03:36Z (C2). No clear source but appears to be within the outflow of the aforementioned CMEs.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: Faint and narrow CME visible to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source appears to be moving/opening field lines just beyond the NE limb in GOES SUVI 284 starting around 2026-03-25T07:16Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: Faint and narrow CME visible to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. This CME appears to overlap the CME first seen in SOHO LASCO C2 at 2026-03-25T09:36Z. The source of this CME appears to be moving/opening field lines visible beyond the NE limb in GOES SUVI 284 starting around 09:00Z, overlaps source movement from CME: 2026-03-25T09:36Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: Bright and slow CME visible to the SE in GOES CCOR-1, and for a frame in SOHO LASCO C2 before a data gap starting at 2026-03-25T12:36Z. It is also visible due S in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is a filament eruption visible along/just beyond the SE limb in GOES SUVI 304 with associated moving/opening field lines in GOES SUVI 284 starting around 2026-03-25T07:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Updated measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
PRELIMINARY parameters based on best fit between one frame of SOHO LASCO C2 and multiple frames in GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: Faint CME to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. The source of this CME appears to be moving/opening field lines visible beyond the NE limb in GOES SUVI 284 starting as early as 09:00Z, responsible for several other overlapping CMEs around this time.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: Faint CME to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. The source of this CME appears to be moving/opening field lines visible beyond the NE limb in GOES SUVI 284 starting as early as 09:00Z, responsible for several other overlapping CMEs around this time.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: Wide, faint, slow to evolve CME observed to the N/NW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, and less wide to the NE in STEREO A COR2. The CME begins very slow and faint, speeding up somewhat as it evolves. The source for this CME is not too clear, but may be associated with a very slow dimming region of the disk centered around N15W05 at 2026-03-25T15:00Z as part of a nearby coronal hole, slowly evolving and drifting with the coronal hole for almost a full 24 hours. This does match the slow, wide nature of this CME front, corroborating it as the potential source along with the swpc_cat triangulation to this general area of the disk.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2, as the CME accelerates after LASCO C2 visibility. The CME front is notably wider in LASCO C3 than STEREO A COR2, so the LASCO C3 width is used.

cmes.note: Faint, thin CME observed to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, not observed in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap. The CME is barely a half width of 10 degrees, perhaps slightly smaller. The source is observed as field line movement over the SW limb beginning at 2026-03-26T23:40Z in GOES SUVI 171/195/284, likely originating from former AR 14392 which is around W100-W105 at this time, although direct observations of the source are not possible without STEREO A EUVI to confirm longitude.

cmeAnalyses.note: Single spacecraft 3D analysis using W100 as an estimation of the source location of former AR 14392 which has rotated around the SW limb, as such the longitude could vary W95-W105. CME half width is barely 10 degrees, perhaps a little smaller.

cmes.note: Faint, wide CME observed to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. The source is associated with an M3.9 flare from AR 14403 which peaked at 2026-03-26T06:23Z, as observed in GOES SUVI 131. Additional source observations include wide field line movement and an EUV wave in GOES SUVI 171/195/284/304 starting around 2026-03-26T06:12Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Bulk analysis of the bright, inner material of this front observed best in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2, which dissipates/blends into the background after its earliest frames. Triangulation puts it about 9 degrees west of the observed source, at E51 with observed E60, but the wide field line movement and EUV wave support this deflection.

Preliminary shock analysis, which may be considered final pending further review, using only SOHO LASCO C2/C3 as the shock front is not observed in STEREO A COR2 for triangulation. As such, the observed source region at E60 is used as a reference and the actual longitude may vary E50-E70. This is a wide analysis including a southern part of the feature believed to be of this same shock front and not a separate event, as the entire feature travels at the same speed through the coronagraphs.

cmes.note: Bright CME seen to the southeast starting near 2026-03-26T19:15Z in GOES CCOR-1 and late in SOHO C2/C3 due to a data gap; the source is likely far-sided as determined from the lack of moving or opening field lines on the Earth-facing disk as seen in GOES coronagraph imagery between 17:00 and 19:00Z. The CME is comprised of a brighter central portion and an asymmetric shock portion which is fainter and overtakes the brighter portion in the imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Updated measurement of the leading edge with SOHO C2/C3 and STEREO A.
Leading edge plane of sky measurement of only the brightest central portion of the CME using GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: Jet CME seen to the northeast in SOHO C2 and SOHO C3, likely associated with a minor eruption with moving field lines seen near or just beyond the northeast limb in GOES SUVI 284 around 2026-03-26T21:00Z

cmeAnalyses.note: Leading edge of the jet with uncertainty in longitude approximately 10 degrees.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the northeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. This event is not seen in STEREO A COR2 imagery due to a data gap from 2026-03-26T16:08Z to 2026-03-27T03:23Z. The source is likely far-sided and related to an opening of field lines observed over the northeast limb in GOES SUVI 171/195/284 imagery starting around 2026-03-26T22:30Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: A plane-of-sky measurement was made using a longitude of -90 degrees due to the far-sided and therefore unknown source location.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the southeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is an eruption observed near the southeast limb starting around 2026-03-27T00:40Z as seen in GOES SUVI 131/171/195/294/304 imagery. An opening of field lines can be seen over the southeast limb at this time in GOES SUVI 171/195/284 imagery and post-eruptive arcades are visible on the southeast limb starting around 2026-03-27T01:00Z in GOES SUVI 131/171/195/304 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the southeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. This event is not observed in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is likely related to an eruption on the southeast limb (centered near S22E80) starting around 2026-03-27T07:00Z as seen in GOES SUVI 131/195/304 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the source location. Since this event is not visible in STEREO A COR2 and a triangulated measurement is therefore not possible, the measured parameters may vary slightly.

cmes.note: Faint CME visible to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. There is a data gap in STEREO A COR2 for this event. The possible source of this CME is seen as moving/opening field lines along the W limb as seen in GOES SUVI 304 starting around 2026-03-27T18:56Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky parameters based on best fit of faint front in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery.

cmes.note: Faint CME visible to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. The CME is partially obscured by the pylon in SOHO LASCO C3. Not visible in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap starting at 2026-03-27T16:53Z. The potential source is a filament eruption starting around 2026-03-27T20:36Z centered around S35E35 as seen in GOES SUVI 195/304.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the potential source location.

cmes.note: Narrow CME visible to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and the NNE in STEREO A COR2. Not seen in GOES CCOR-1 due to Earthshine. The likely source is a partial filament eruption seen in GOES SUVI 304 starting at 2026-03-27T21:56Z around N15W55. There is possible deflection from the source location within the field of view.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT as well as the source location.

cmes.note: Wide CME visible to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. The CME is also visible in GOES CCOR-1 starting at 2026-03-28T06:45Z due to Earthshine. The source is a long duration M1.3 flare from AR 14405 (approximately S25E70) starting at 2026-03-28T02:16Z. Dimming and EUV wave are best seen in GOES SUVI 195. Moving field lines are also visible in GOES SUVI 171/195/284. The eruption can also be seen off the east limb in STEREO A EUV 195 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 in SWPC_CAT.

cmes.note: CME visible to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. The CME is partially obscured by the pylon in SOHO LASCO C3. The source is an eruption with dimming starting around 2026-03-28T07:04Z as seen in GOES SUVI 195 imagery around S30E55, east of AR 4405.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery as well as the source location.

cmes.note: CME visible to the NE in STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1. Not initially visible in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery in real time due to a data gap. The potential source is a C4.1 flare starting at 2026-03-28T11:18Z from Active Region 14404 (approximately N15E55). The eruption can be best seen as brightening and opening field lines in GOES SUVI 195 imagery. Opening field lines are also visible in GOES SUVI 284 imagery. The eruption is visible beyond the NE limb in STEREO A EUV 195 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Preliminary measurement based on limited available STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery.

cmes.note: Narrow CME first seen to the SE in STEREO A COR2 starting at 2026-03-28T17:09Z. It is also seen to the SE in GOES CCOR-1 but is covered by a real-time data gap in SOHO LASCO C2/C3. The source of this CME is most likely an eruption best seen in GOES SUVI 284 from AR 4405 (S25E56) starting at 2026-03-28T14:56Z with opening field lines. Likely due to its proximity to a coronal hole centered approximately around S15E35 at the CME start time, there is some deflection.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit between STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in SWPC_CAT as well as visible source candidate.

cmes.note: Narrow CME first seen to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-03-28T20:12Z. It is also seen to the SE in GOES CCOR-1 but is obscured by the pylon in SOHO LASCO C3. It is covered by a real-time data gap in STEREO A COR2. This CME is very faint and is best seen in running difference imagery. There is no good source candidate on the Earth-facing disk, so that source is possibly far-sided.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement based on running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: Narrow CME first seen to the ESE in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-03-28T20:24Z. It is also seen to the ESE in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 but is covered by a real-time data gap in STEREO A COR2. This CME is very faint and is best seen in running difference imagery. There is no good source candidate on the Earth-facing disk, so that source is possibly far-sided.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement based on running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: Narrow CME first seen to the SE in GOES CCOR-1 starting around 2026-03-28T23:45Z. It is covered by real-time data gaps in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. This feature is very faint and is best seen in running difference imagery. There is not a good source candidate on the Earth-facing disk, so the source is possibly far-sided.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement based on running difference imagery from GOES CCOR-1.
